page.title: Nominalisation and Formal Register
page.subtitle_html: Nominalisation and Formal Register - C1 noun-based style, formal verbs, and controlled academic phrasing.
page.subtitle_text: C1 noun-based style, formal verbs, and controlled academic phrasing.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Nominalisation and Formal Register: compact anchors for turning actions into formal noun phrases.","rules":[{"label":"Action to noun","text":"Use a noun phrase when formal style needs distance or abstraction.","example":"The government made a decision to reduce expenditure."},{"label":"Formal verbs","text":"Choose verbs such as conduct, implement, indicate, and require in formal contexts.","example":"The committee conducted an evaluation of the proposal."},{"label":"Controlled register","text":"Avoid casual phrasing when an academic or professional tone is expected.","example":"Further analysis is required before publication."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the English Sentence Builder C1 nominalisation and formal register lesson package.
